title: Encoded PowerShell from Office parent
description: Flags PowerShell launched with -EncodedCommand / hidden window spawned by an Office application — a common initial-access and execution technique surfaced from alert A-1042 and validated by Atomic Red Team test T1059.001.
sigma:
title: Encoded PowerShell From Office Parent
id: 7e2b6c10-2f9a-4d1e-9a11-encoded-ps
status: experimental
description: Detects PowerShell launched with an encoded/hidden command from an Office process
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1059/001/
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '-enc'
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - '-w hidden'
      - '-nop'
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Admin automation tooling
level: high
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName =~ "powershell.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-enc", "-EncodedCommand", "-w hidden", "-nop")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe", "excel.exe", "outlook.exe")

title: Suspicious OAuth consent to unverified app
description: Detects users granting high-privilege mailbox scopes to recently-registered, unverified third-party applications (from A-1088).
sigma:
title: OAuth Consent To Unverified Application
id: 91a0c8de-1b22-4f0a-aa31-oauth-consent
status: experimental
description: High-privilege mailbox scopes granted to an unverified app
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit
detection:
  selection:
    Operation: 'Consent to application'
    Scopes|contains:
      - 'Mail.ReadWrite'
      - 'offline_access'
      - 'Mail.Send'
    PublisherVerified: false
  condition: selection
level: medium
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1098.003
kql:
CloudAppEvents
| where ActionType == "Consent to application"
| where RawEventData.PublisherVerified == false

title: LSASS credential access via comsvcs.dll
description: Identifies process handle access to lsass.exe paired with comsvcs.dll MiniDump (T1003.001), derived from critical alert A-1190 and validated by Atomic Red Team.
sigma:
title: LSASS Dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump
id: 3f5d9a21-77bc-4e0a-bb90-lsass-comsvcs
status: stable
description: Detects credential dumping of LSASS using comsvcs.dll MiniDump
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1003/001/
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'comsvcs.dll'
      - 'MiniDump'
  selection_alt:
    CommandLine|contains: '#24'
    CommandLine|contains: 'lsass'
  condition: selection or selection_alt
level: critical
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDump", "#24")

title: Impossible travel for privileged accounts
description: Correlates sign-ins from geographically distant locations within an implausible time window for finance/admin roles (A-1205).
sigma:
title: Impossible Travel For Privileged Account
id: a4c1b2e3-9d88-4a10-9c21-impossible-travel
status: experimental
description: Successful sign-ins from distant locations within a short window
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    ResultType: 0
    RiskState: 'atRisk'
    RiskEventTypes|contains: 'impossibleTravel'
  condition: selection
level: high
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1078.004
kql:
SigninLogs
| where ResultType == 0
| where RiskEventTypes has "impossibleTravel"

title: Scheduled task persistence from TEMP
description: Surfaces scheduled tasks whose action executes a script located in a temporary directory (A-1233).
sigma:
title: Scheduled Task Created From TEMP
id: c2e7f1a9-44bb-4d10-a8e1-schtask-temp
status: experimental
description: schtasks creating a task that runs a script from a temp directory
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\schtasks.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '/create'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\Temp\'
      - '%TEMP%'
      - 'AppData\Local\Temp'
  condition: selection
level: medium
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1053.005
kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "/create" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\Temp\\", "%TEMP%")
rejectReason: Too noisy — legitimate installers create temp tasks. Needs tighter scoping before re-proposing.

title: Mass file rename / ransomware canary
description: Detects high-velocity file renames to a uniform extension combined with shadow copy deletion (A-1310).
sigma:
title: Shadow Copy Deletion Before Mass Encryption
id: d9b3a7c1-5e22-4a90-bb31-ransomware-canary
status: experimental
description: vssadmin deleting shadow copies, a precursor to ransomware encryption
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1490/
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
level: critical
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.t1486
kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete", "shadows")

title: Periodic C2 beacon detection
description: Flags regular outbound connections with low jitter to threat-intel listed ranges (A-1377).
sigma:
title: Periodic Beaconing To Public IP
id: e1f4b8d2-6a33-4b20-9c41-c2-beacon
status: experimental
description: Regular outbound connections consistent with C2 beaconing
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Initiated: true
    DestinationIsIpv6: false
    DestinationPort:
      - 443
      - 80
  timeframe: 5m
  condition: selection | count() by DestinationIp > 30
level: high
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1071.001
kql:
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| summarize Connections = count() by DeviceName, RemoteIP
| where Connections > 30

title: Kerberoasting — bulk RC4 TGS requests
description: Detects a single host requesting many RC4-encrypted service tickets (SPNs), consistent with Kerberoasting against Active Directory (A-1402).
sigma:
title: Potential Kerberoasting via RC4 Ticket Requests
id: b7c2e9a4-3d11-4f80-9a22-kerberoast
status: experimental
description: Many RC4 (0x17) TGS requests from one account, indicating Kerberoasting
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1558/003/
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4769
    TicketEncryptionType: '0x17'
    TicketOptions: '0x40810000'
  timeframe: 10m
  condition: selection | count() by AccountName > 10
level: medium
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1558.003
kql:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4769 and TicketEncryptionType == "0x17"
| summarize Requests = count() by AccountName, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where Requests > 10
